name: node-buffer
title: node-buffer
description:
Node.js Buffer API for browserify
node-buffer is a port of well known buffer API of nodejs to browser context.
.
Using require('buffer')` or the `Buffer` global variable will allow
browserify program to include automatically this module on your code.
Thus any npm module using buffer features will work in the browser.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-buffer because Node.js Buffer API for browserify.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-buffer as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-buffer with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-buffer --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
